memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, const Triangulation &t, const MeshBuilder::BuildSettings &settings={}, ProgressCallback cb={})
memberdef.name: fromTriangles

briefdescription.para: construct mesh from vertex coordinates and a set of triangles with given ids
points of triMesh will be moves in the result
construct mesh from TriMesh representation

memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, Triangulation &t, std::vector< MeshBuilder::VertDuplication > *dups=nullptr, const MeshBuilder::BuildSettings &settings={})
memberdef.name: fromTrianglesDuplicatingNonManifoldVertices

detaileddescription.para: construct mesh from vertex coordinates and a set of triangles with given ids; unlike simple fromTriangles() it tries to resolve non-manifold vertices by creating duplicate vertices

memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, const std::vector< VertId > &verts, const Vector< MeshBuilder::VertSpan, FaceId > &faces, const MeshBuilder::BuildSettings &settings={}, ProgressCallback cb={})
memberdef.name: fromFaceSoup

detaileddescription.para: construct mesh from vertex coordinates and construct mesh topology from face soup, where each face can have arbitrary degree (not only triangles); all non-triangular faces will be automatically subdivided on triangles

briefdescription.para: compare that two meshes are exactly the same
returns coordinates of the edge origin
returns coordinates of the edge destination
returns vector equal to edge destination point minus edge origin point
returns line segment of given edge
returns a point on the edge: origin point for f=0 and destination point for f=1
computes coordinates of point given as edge and relative position on it
computes the center of given edge
returns three points of left face of e: v0 = orgPnt( e ), v1 = destPnt( e )

detaileddescription.para: returns three points of left face of e: v[0] = orgPnt( e ), v[1] = destPnt( e ) This one is not in the bindings because of the reference-to-array parameter.

briefdescription.para: returns three points of left face of e: res[0] = orgPnt( e ), res[1] = destPnt( e )
returns three points of given face

detaileddescription.para: returns three points of given face This one is not in the bindings because of the reference-to-array parameter.

briefdescription.para: returns three points of given face
computes coordinates of point given as face and barycentric representation
returns the centroid of given triangle
returns aspect ratio of given mesh triangle equal to the ratio of the circum-radius to twice its in-radius
returns squared circumcircle diameter of given mesh triangle
returns circumcircle diameter of given mesh triangle
converts vertex into barycentric representation
converts face id and 3d point into barycentric representation
converts face id and 3d point into barycentric representation
converts vertex into edge-point representation
converts edge and 3d point into edge-point representation
returns one of three face vertices, closest to given point
returns one of three face vertices, closest to given point
returns one of three face edges, closest to given point
returns one of three face edges, closest to given point
returns Euclidean length of the edge
computes and returns the lengths of all edges in the mesh
returns squared Euclidean length of the edge (faster to compute than length)
computes directed double area of left triangular face of given edge
computes directed double area for a triangular face from its vertices
returns twice the area of given face
returns the area of given face

detaileddescription.para: computes the area of given face-region This is skipped in the bindings because it conflicts with the overload taking a pointer in C#. Since that overload is strictly more useful, we're keeping that one.

briefdescription.para: computes the area of given face-region (or whole mesh)

detaileddescription.para: computes the sum of directed areas for faces from given region This is skipped in the bindings because it conflicts with the overload taking a pointer in C#. Since that overload is strictly more useful, we're keeping that one.

briefdescription.para: computes the sum of directed areas for faces from given region (or whole mesh)

detaileddescription.para: computes the sum of absolute projected area of faces from given region as visible if look from given direction This is skipped in the bindings because it conflicts with the overload taking a pointer in C#. Since that overload is strictly more useful, we're keeping that one.

briefdescription.para: computes the sum of absolute projected area of faces from given region (or whole mesh) as visible if look from given direction

detaileddescription.para: returns volume of the object surrounded by given region (or whole mesh if (region) is nullptr); if the region has holes then each hole will be virtually filled by adding triangles for each edge and the hole's geometrical center

briefdescription.para: computes the perimeter of the hole specified by one of its edges with no valid left face (left is hole)

detaileddescription.para: computes directed area of the hole specified by one of its edges with no valid left face (left is hole); if the hole is planar then returned vector is orthogonal to the plane pointing outside and its magnitude is equal to hole area

briefdescription.para: computes unit vector that is both orthogonal to given edge and to the normal of its left triangle, the vector is directed inside left triangle
computes triangular face normal from its vertices
computes triangular face normal from its vertices
returns the plane containing given triangular face with normal looking outwards
computes sum of directed double areas of all triangles around given vertex
computes the length of summed directed double areas of all triangles around given vertex
computes normal in a vertex using sum of directed areas of neighboring triangles

detaileddescription.para: computes normal in three vertices of p's triangle, then interpolates them using barycentric coordinates and normalizes again; this is the same normal as in rendering with smooth shading
computes angle-weighted sum of normals of incident faces of given vertex (only (region) faces will be considered); the sum is normalized before returning

briefdescription.para: computes normalized half sum of face normals sharing given edge (only (region) faces will be considered);

detaileddescription.para: returns pseudonormal in corresponding face/edge/vertex for signed distance calculation as suggested in the article "Signed Distance Computation Using the Angle Weighted Pseudonormal" by J. Andreas Baerentzen and Henrik Aanaes, https:/// backend.orbit.dtu.dk/ws/portalfiles/portal/3977815/B_rentzen.pdf unlike normal( const MeshTriPoint & p ), this is not a smooth function

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &pt, const MeshProjectionResult &proj, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: signedDistance

detaileddescription.para:
given a point (pt) in 3D and the closest point to in on mesh (proj),
signed distance from pt to mesh: positive value - outside mesh, negative - inside mesh; this method can return wrong sign if the closest point is located on self-intersecting part of the mesh

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &pt, const MeshTriPoint &proj, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: signedDistance

detaileddescription.para:
given a point (pt) in 3D, computes the closest point on mesh, and
signed distance from pt to mesh: positive value - outside mesh, negative - inside mesh; this method can return wrong sign if the closest point is located on self-intersecting part of the mesh

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &pt, float maxDistSq, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: signedDistance

detaileddescription.para:
given a point (pt) in 3D, computes the closest point on mesh, and
signed distance from pt to mesh: positive value - outside mesh, negative - inside mesh; or std::nullopt if the projection point is not within maxDist; this method can return wrong sign if the closest point is located on self-intersecting part of the mesh

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &pt, float windingNumberThreshold=0.5f, float beta=2) const
memberdef.name: isOutside

detaileddescription.para:
computes whether a point (pt) is located outside the object surrounded by this mesh using generalized winding number
beta
determines the precision of winding number computation: the more the better, recommended value 2 or more

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &pt, const MeshProjectionResult &proj, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: isOutsideByProjNorm

detaileddescription.para: computes whether a point (pt) is located outside the object surrounded by this mesh using pseudonormal at the closest point to in on mesh (proj); this method works much faster than isOutside but can return wrong sign if the closest point is located on self-intersecting part of the mesh

briefdescription.para: computes the sum of triangle angles at given vertex; optionally returns whether the vertex is on boundary

memberdef.argsstring: (float minSumAngle, const VertBitSet *region=nullptr, const ProgressCallback &cb={}) const
memberdef.name: findSpikeVertices

briefdescription.para: returns vertices where the sum of triangle angles is below given threshold

detaileddescription.para: given an edge between two triangular faces, computes sine of dihedral angle between them: 0 if both faces are in the same plane, positive if the faces form convex surface, negative if the faces form concave surface

given an edge between two triangular faces, computes cosine of dihedral angle between them: 1 if both faces are in the same plane, 0 if the surface makes right angle turn at the edge, -1 if the faces overlap one another

given an edge between two triangular faces, computes the dihedral angle between them: 0 if both faces are in the same plane, positive if the faces form convex surface, negative if the faces form concave surface; please consider the usage of faster dihedralAngleSin(e) and dihedralAngleCos(e)
computes discrete mean curvature in given vertex, measures in length^-1; 0 for planar regions, positive for convex surface, negative for concave surface
computes discrete mean curvature in given edge, measures in length^-1; 0 for planar regions, positive for convex surface, negative for concave surface

computes discrete Gaussian curvature (or angle defect) at given vertex, which 0 in inner vertices on planar mesh parts and reaches 2*pi on needle's tip, see http:/// math.uchicago.edu/~may/REU2015/REUPapers/Upadhyay.pdf optionally returns whether the vertex is on boundary

briefdescription.para: finds all mesh edges where dihedral angle is distinct from planar PI angle on at least given value

detaileddescription.para: computes cotangent of the angle in the left( e ) triangle opposite to e, and returns 0 if left face does not exist
computes sum of cotangents of the angle in the left and right triangles opposite to given edge, consider cotangents zero for not existing triangles

memberdef.argsstring: (VertId v, bool angleWeigted, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const UndirectedEdgeBitSet *creases=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: quadraticForm

detaileddescription.para: computes quadratic form in the vertex as the sum of squared distances from 1) planes of adjacent triangles, with the weight equal to the angle of adjacent triangle at this vertex divided on PI in case of angleWeigted=true; 2) lines of adjacent boundary and crease edges
returns the bounding box containing all valid vertices (implemented via getAABBTree()) this bounding box is insignificantly bigger that minimal box due to AABB algorithms precision
passes through all valid vertices and finds the minimal bounding box containing all of them; if toWorld transformation is given then returns minimal bounding box in world space

passes through all given faces (or whole mesh if region == null) and finds the minimal bounding box containing all of them if toWorld transformation is given then returns minimal bounding box in world space

briefdescription.para: computes average length of an edge in this mesh
computes average position of all valid mesh vertices
computes center of mass considering that density of all triangles is the same
computes bounding box and returns its center
for all points not in topology.getValidVerts() sets coordinates to (0,0,0)

detaileddescription.para: applies given transformation to specified vertices if region is nullptr, all valid mesh vertices are used

briefdescription.para: creates new point and assigns given position to it

detaileddescription.para: append points to mesh and connect them as closed edge loop returns first EdgeId of new edges
append points to mesh and connect them returns first EdgeId of new edges

memberdef.argsstring: (EdgeId first, EdgeId last, const std::vector< Vector3f > &contourPoints)
memberdef.name: attachEdgeLoopPart

detaileddescription.para: append points to mesh and connect them to given edges making edge loop first point connects with first edge dest last point connects with last edge org note that first and last edge should have no left face
split given edge on two parts: dest(returned-edge) = org(e) - newly created vertex, org(returned-edge) = org(e-before-split), dest(e) = dest(e-before-split)

left and right faces of given edge if valid are also subdivided on two parts each; the split edge will keep both face IDs and their degrees, and the new edge will have new face IDs and new faces are triangular; if left or right faces of the original edge were in the region, then include new parts of these faces in the region
new2Old
receive mapping from newly appeared triangle to its original triangle (part to full)

briefdescription.para: same, but split given edge on two equal parts

detaileddescription.para:
split given triangle on three triangles, introducing new vertex with given coordinates and connecting it to original triangle vertices; if region is given, then it must include (f) and new faces will be added there as well
new2Old
receive mapping from newly appeared triangle to its original triangle (part to full)

briefdescription.para: same, putting new vertex in the centroid of original triangle
appends another mesh as separate connected component(s) to this

memberdef.argsstring: (const Mesh &from, FaceMap *outFmap, VertMap *outVmap=nullptr, WholeEdgeMap *outEmap=nullptr, bool rearrangeTriangles=false)
memberdef.name: addMesh

briefdescription.para: optionally returns mappings: from.id -> this.id

memberdef.argsstring: (const Mesh &from, FaceMap *outFmap=nullptr, VertMap *outVmap=nullptr, WholeEdgeMap *outEmap=nullptr, bool rearrangeTriangles=false)
memberdef.name: addPart

briefdescription.para: appends whole or part of another mesh as separate connected component(s) to this

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshPart &from, bool flipOrientation=false, const std::vector< EdgePath > &thisContours={}, const std::vector< EdgePath > &fromContours={}, PartMapping map={})
memberdef.name: addMeshPart

briefdescription.para: contours on this mesh that have to be stitched with
contours on from mesh during addition optionally returns mappings: from.id -> this.id

detaileddescription.para:
appends whole or part of another mesh to this joining added faces with existed ones along given contours
flipOrientation
true means that every (from) triangle is inverted before adding

memberdef.argsstring: (const Mesh &from, const FaceBitSet &fromFaces, bool flipOrientation=false, const std::vector< EdgePath > &thisContours={}, const std::vector< EdgePath > &fromContours={}, const PartMapping &map={})
memberdef.name: addPartByMask

memberdef.argsstring: (const FaceBitSet &region, bool flipOrientation=false, const PartMapping &map={}) const
memberdef.name: cloneRegion

briefdescription.para: creates new mesh from given triangles of this mesh
tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces, vertices and points
optionally returns mappings: old.id -> new.id

detaileddescription.para: tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces, vertices and points, reorder all faces, vertices and edges according to given maps, each containing old id -> new id mapping

packs tightly and rearranges vertices, triangles and edges to put close in space elements in close indices
preserveAABBTree
whether to keep valid mesh's AABB tree after return (it will take longer to compute and it will occupy more memory)

briefdescription.para: deletes multiple given faces, also deletes adjacent edges and vertices if they were not shared by remaining faces and not in

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &point, PointOnFace &res, float maxDistSq=FLT_MAX, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const AffineXf3f *xf=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: projectPoint

detaileddescription.para:
finds the closest mesh point on this mesh (or its region) to given point;
point
source location to look the closest to
res
found closest point including Euclidean coordinates and FaceId
maxDistSq
search only in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point, smaller value here increases performance
xf
is mesh-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed and works much faster;
false if no mesh point is found in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &point, MeshProjectionResult &res, float maxDistSq=FLT_MAX, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const AffineXf3f *xf=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: projectPoint

detaileddescription.para:
finds the closest mesh point on this mesh (or its region) to given point;
point
source location to look the closest to
res
found closest point including Euclidean coordinates, barycentric coordinates, FaceId and squared distance to point
maxDistSq
search only in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point, smaller value here increases performance
xf
is mesh-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed and works much faster;
false if no mesh point is found in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &point, MeshProjectionResult &res, float maxDistSq=FLT_MAX, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const AffineXf3f *xf=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: findClosestPoint

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &point, float maxDistSq=FLT_MAX, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const AffineXf3f *xf=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: projectPoint

detaileddescription.para:
finds the closest mesh point on this mesh (or its region) to given point;
point
source location to look the closest to
maxDistSq
search only in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point, smaller value here increases performance
xf
is mesh-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed and works much faster;
found closest point including Euclidean coordinates, barycentric coordinates, FaceId and squared distance to point or std::nullopt if no mesh point is found in the ball with sqrt(maxDistSq) radius around given point

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3f &point, float maxDistSq=FLT_MAX, const FaceBitSet *region=nullptr, const AffineXf3f *xf=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: findClosestPoint

briefdescription.para: returns cached aabb-tree for this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached aabb-tree for this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist
returns cached aabb-tree for points of this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached aabb-tree for points of this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist
returns cached dipoles of aabb-tree nodes for this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached dipoles of aabb-tree nodes for this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist

detaileddescription.para:
invalidates caches (aabb-trees) after any change in mesh geometry or topology
pointsChanged
specifies whether points have changed (otherwise only topology has changed)

updates existing caches in case of few vertices were changed insignificantly, and topology remained unchanged; it shall be considered as a faster alternative to invalidateCaches() and following rebuild of trees

briefdescription.para: returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
requests the removal of unused capacity
reflects the mesh from a given plane

detaileddescription.para: This class represents a mesh, including topology (connectivity) information and point coordinates, as well as some caches to accelerate search algorithms
